GENERAL COUNSEL

December 12, 1986 
Ms. Colleen McGee 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
428 J Street, 8th Floor 
Sacramento, California 
95814 
Dear Ms. McGee: 
MEMBER RAILROADS 
SOUTHERN PACIFIC TRANSPORTATION 
COMPANY 
THE ATCHISON. TOPEKA & SANTA FE 
RAILWAY COMPANY 
UNION PACIFIC RA!LROAD COMPANY 
BURLINGTON NORTHERN, INC. 
This is to confirm our conversation of October 16, 
1986, in which you stated that the California Railroad 
Association, which is ceasing operations, and Herbert w. 
Nobriga, who will be terminating the Association, need 
not file a reregistration form or a termination form 
for the 1987-88 Legislative Session, as they will be 
automatically terminated. 
(MH) 
FPPC File No. A-86-339 
Sincerely, 
Myra C. Herd 
Office Manager 
Confirmed by telephone January 8, 1987. 
I

Dear Ms. Herd: 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform 
Act was received on December 18, 1986 by the Fair Political 
Practices Commission. 
If you have any questions about your 
advice request, you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, 
or more information is needed, you should expect a response 
within 21 working days. 
You also should be aware that your 
letter and our response are public records which may be 
disclosed to the public upon receipt of a proper request for 
disclosure.

sections.conclusion: Based on a conversation between Herbert W. Nobriga and Ms. Colleen McGee on October 16, 1986, the California Railroad Association, which is ceasing operations, and Herbert W. Nobriga, who will be terminating the Association, need not file a reregistration form or a termination form for the 1987-88 Legislative Session, as they will be automatically terminated.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether the California Railroad Association and its General Counsel Herbert W. Nobriga are required to file reregistration or termination forms when the Association ceases operations.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The California Railroad Association and Herbert W. Nobriga are not required to file reregistration or termination forms for the 1987-88 Legislative Session because they will be automatically terminated upon cessation of the Association's operations.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes both an incoming request letter from California Railroad Association dated December 12, 1986, and an FPPC acknowledgment letter dated December 22, 1986. The advice was given verbally on October 16, 1986, and this document confirms that conversation. FPPC File No. A-86-339. The acknowledgment letter indicates this is a formal advice request being processed.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether the California Railroad Association and its General Counsel Herbert W. Nobriga are required to file reregistration or termination forms when the Association ceases operations.

CONCLUSION: Based on a conversation between Herbert W. Nobriga and Ms. Colleen McGee on October 16, 1986, the California Railroad Association, which is ceasing operations, and Herbert W. Nobriga, who will be terminating the Association, need not file a reregistration form or a termination form for the 1987-88 Legislative Session, as they will be automatically terminated.

embedding.summary: This document confirms an FPPC verbal advice regarding whether the California Railroad Association and its General Counsel need to file reregistration or termination forms when the Association ceases operations. The FPPC advised that no such forms are required as the Association will be automatically terminated.
